title: Rich Greek Ground Meat Baked Pastitsio
description: A hearty, comforting Greek dish made with ground meat, bechamel sauce, and a rich tomato sauce, perfect for a weeknight dinner. This recipe makes 6 servings and can be prepared in advance for a stress-free meal.
instructions: Cook the ground lamb and suet in a large pan over medium-high heat, breaking it up with a spoon as it cooks, until browned and crispy, about 10 minutes.
Add the chopped onion, salt, and pepper to the pan and cook until the onion is translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the all-purpose flour to the pan and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.

Add the crushed tomatoes, tomato paste, red wine (if using), and glace cherries to the pan and stir to combine. Bring the sauce to a simmer and cook, stirring occasionally, until it has thickened and reduced slightly, about 20 minutes.
While the sauce is cooking, prepare the bechamel sauce.

To assemble the pastitsio, spread a layer of the meat sauce in the bottom of a 9x13-inch baking dish. Top with a layer of the bechamel sauce, followed by a layer of the grated Parmesan cheese. Repeat this process, ending with a layer of bechamel sauce on top.
Dot the top of the pastitsio with the butter and sprinkle with the chopped parsley.
Bake the pastitsio in the preheated oven for 35-40 minutes, or until the top is golden brown and the sauce is bubbly.
Let the pastitsio rest for 10-15 minutes before serving.

Note: To make the bechamel sauce, melt 2 tablespoons of butter in a small saucepan over medium heat. Whisk in 2 tablespoons of all-purpose flour and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly. Slowly whisk in 1 cup of milk and cook, stirring constantly, until the sauce thickens and comes to a simmer. Remove from heat and stir in 1 egg and a pinch of salt and pepper. Set aside until ready to use.
